channel.title: Joshua Berry on Berrytree.org
channel.description: Recent content in Joshua Berry on Berrytree.org

item.title: Chapter 1: Williamsons, Fosters, Berrys & Kemps
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Preface | Table of Contents | Chapter 2: Robert Berry →

Chapter 1#

Williamsons, Fosters, Berrys & Kemps#

I have been researching the Robert Berry family from Orange County since 1997. When I first started researching, I found that the Census forms from 1790 to 1840, only listed the head of household’s given and surnames. Sometimes they would be listed alphabetically, which was not totally useless but not very helpful either. When the families were listed in the order of farms and plantation physical location, the information became more valuable. I would search the census forms from the very early years and become familiar with as many of the surnames located near one of my known ancestors as possible. Soon I learned that a cluster of surnames in one county would often vary greatly from the surnames listed in the very next county. Since the population was small, clusters of surnames in a close proximity were often related to each other. As our ancestors relocated from one state to another, the pattern was repeated over and over. When I located a family surname who seemed to be a relative from a previous community, I started looking at the surnames of his neighbors. I always paid particular attention to these very close neighbors. This is a very important tool; since there were really not very many people in these early settlements, many of them migrated there together. Many of them were friends and a lot of them were relatives and children who had known each other their entire lives. They had grown up together and when the time came to relocate, they chose to go together.

item.title: Franklin Pierce Berry
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Franklin Pierce Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Joshua Berry × Nancy Ellison — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 4Lewis Berry × Sally Toler — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 5Ira Berry × Nancy Wynn — NC and Kentucky
Gen 6Franklin Pierce Berry × Sophia Sellers — Kentucky → Illinois

Generation 6 Franklin Pierce Berry

Born
July 12, 1854, Uniontown, Kentucky
Died
January 1, 1909, Williamson County, Illinois
Married
Sophia M. Sellers (married February 6, 1878, died 1912)
Parents
Ira Berry and Nancy Wynn Berry, Kentucky
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Franklin Pierce Berry#

Franklin Pierce Berry was born on July 12th, 1854 in Uniontown Kentucky and died on January 1st, 1909 in Williamson Co., Illinois. On February 6th, 1878 Franklin P. Berry married Sophia M. Sellers in Evansville, Indiana. Sophie was the daughter of Orman Sellers and Louisa Ashby. She was born on April 6th, 1860 in Anton, Kentucky, and died in 1912.

item.title: Kate Berry
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Kate Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Joshua Berry × Nancy Ellison — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 4Lewis Berry × Sally Toler — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 5Ira Berry × Nancy Wynn — NC and Kentucky
Gen 6Franklin Pierce Berry × Sophie Sellers — Kentucky
Gen 7Kate Berry × Benjamin Franklin Moake — Kentucky → Williamson County, Illinois

Generation 7 Kate Berry

Born
August 12, 1896, Sebree, Webster County, Kentucky
Died
August 22, 1966, Williamson County, Illinois
Married
Benjamin Franklin Moake (born August 18, 1892, died February 9, 1979)
Parents
Franklin Pierce Berry and Sophia M. Sellers Berry, Kentucky
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Kate Berry#

Kate Berry Was born Aug 12, 1896 Sebree, Webster Co., Ky, she died Aug 22, 1966 and is buried at Lake Creek Cemetery, Williamson Co., Illinois. Kate married Benjamin Franklin Moake in Williamson Co, Illinois, son of Eziekel Moake and Dell Everett. He was born August 18, 1892 in Lake Creek, Williamson Co., Illinois, and died February 09, 1979 in Williamson Co., Illinois. In 1941, Kate had her birth certificate replaced, the witnesses for her were A.M. Sellers, an uncle age 75, Samuel Hansford husband of Rebecca Sellers and G.W. Bronson, a non relative age 80 years. The certificate is listed as #175914 in the bureau of vital statistics, Commonwealth of Kentucky, Webster Co.

item.title: Chapter 2: Robert Berry 1729–1814
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Chapter 1: Williamsons, Fosters, Berrys, Kemps | Table of Contents | Chapter 3: Mary Berry →

Chapter 2#

Robert Berry 1729–1814#

Robert Berry OC came to a part of Granville County, North Carolina, which was soon to become Orange County, NC. We know that he built a small cabin in the northeast corner of his staked out land. This cabin was documented in Robert Berry’s OC Entry for his Land Grant. Thomas Bradford owned the land just north of Robert Berry’s OC claim.

item.title: Ira Berry (NC)
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Ira Berry (NC)
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Joshua Berry × Nancy Ellison — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 4Lewis Berry × Sally Toler — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 5Ira Berry × Nancy Wynn — Orange County, NC → Union County, KY

Generation 5 Ira Berry

Born
1833, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
c. 1864–1870 (probably killed in the Civil War)
Married
Nancy Wynn/Winn — married before 1856, probably in Tennessee or Kentucky
Parents
Lewis Berry and Sally (Sarah) Toler Berry, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Ira Berry#

Ira Berry was born in 1833 in Orange County, North Carolina, the son of Lewis Berry and Sally (Sarah) Toler Berry. Lewis and Sally were married on October 13, 1829, in Orange County, NC.

Book Chapter: Read the full story in Chapter 6: Joshua Berry from Our Berrys in Frontier America

item.title: Chapter 6: Joshua Berry 1766–1838
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Chapter 5: John Berry | Table of Contents | Chapter 7: Isaac Berry →

Ancestor Profile: Joshua Berry Sr. — view the full family profile on berrytree.org

Chapter 6#

Joshua Berry 1766–1838#

Joshua Berry was probably the fourth child of Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry. He was named for his mother’s oldest brother, Joshua Cate. I think Joshua was born around 1766, and I know his wife, Nancy Ellison, was born in 1775 based on census information taken from the 1850 Orange County, North Carolina, census. Joshua Berry, with his family and most of his descendants, is one of the four brothers whose families remained in Orange County, North Carolina.

item.title: Joshua Berry Jr. Ohio
item.description:
Joshua Berry Jr. (1801–1879)

Son of Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry, migrated from Orange County, NC, to Ohio, Indiana, and Iowa.

Joshua Berry Jr., Son of Joshua Berry and Nancy Ellison Berry#

Ancestors:

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia

item.title: Robert Berry (OC)
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Robert Berry (OC)
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina

Generation 2 Robert Berry

Born
c. 1729, Princess Anne County, Virginia
Died
1814, Orange County, North Carolina
Married
Elizabeth Cate — married c. 1759, Orange County, NC
Parents
Robert Berry and Mary Williamson, Princess Anne County, VA
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Robert Berry#

Robert Berry was the son of Robert Berry and Mary Williamson of Princess Anne County, Virginia, and he came to Orange County, North Carolina, in 1751.

item.title: Elizabeth Cate Berry
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Elizabeth Cate Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina

Generation 2 (by marriage) Elizabeth Cate Berry

Born
c. 1735, Virginia
Died
c. 1817, Orange County, North Carolina
Married
Robert Berry — married c. 1759, Orange County, NC
Parents
John and Margery Lawrence Cate, Prince George County, Virginia
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Elizabeth Cate Berry#

Elizabeth Cate was the daughter of John and Margery Cate. Her oldest brother was Joshua Cate and her youngest brother was Thomas Cate, who was a chain carrier when Robert Berry’s Granville Grant was surveyed in 1753. Robert Berry and Elizabeth Cate were married about 1759. At least three of their children were already married before marriage records were kept in Hillsborough. The ages of their children would indicate that their marriage took place around 1759.

Cates Crest

item.title: Robert Berry (PAC)
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Robert Berry (PAC)
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina

Generation 1 Robert Berry (PAC)

Born
Unknown — probably born in England
Died
c. 1730, Princess Anne County, Virginia
Married
Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Parents
Unknown — most likely born in England
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Robert Berry (PAC)#

Robert Berry (PAC) of South Norfolk County, Virginia, married Mary Williamson and established a home in Princess Anne County, Virginia, which is now known as Virginia Beach, Virginia. Robert and Mary had two sons. The oldest was Richard Berry and the baby was Robert Berry “Orange County, NC” (OC) who was born about 1729. Robert Berry (PAC) wrote his will in 1730 and died shortly after Robert Berry (Orange County, NC) was born.

item.title: Roger Williamson PAC
item.description:
Roger Williamson & Ann Foster

Early settlers of Princess Anne County, Virginia, from England. Earliest known Berry ancestors in America.

Roger Williamson from England and Princess Ann County, Virginia#

PARENTS of Roger Williamson and Ann Foster are unknown at this time.

Likely Source of Robert Berry (PAC) and Mary Williamson’s Relatives in England#

St. Botolph Church is located on Aldersgate Street in London, England

August 22, 1663

item.title: Robert Berry Ancestors
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Robert Berry (OC)
  4. Ancestors

Generation 2 Robert Berry (OC)

Born
1729, Princess Anne County, Virginia
Died
1814, Fiddleton Plantation, Orange County, NC
Married
Elizabeth Cate
Parents
Robert Berry (PAC) and Mary Williamson
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Ancestors of Robert Berry (OC)#

Maternal Ancestors#

  • Great Grandparents: George and Mary Hutchinson Kempe
  • Great Grandparents: Roger and Ann Foster Williamson
  • Grandparents: Richard and Mary Kempe Williamson
  • Parents: Robert and Mary Williamson Berry

Descendants of Ann Foster and Roger Williamson#

  • Gen 1 Ann Foster (b. 1620, England — d. c. 1692, Parish Church, Lynnhaven Parish, Lower Norfolk County, Virginia)
      • Roger Williamson (b. Unknown — m. June 5, 1646, Virginia — d. bef. 1650, Lower Norfolk Co., VA)
    • ——–Gen 2 Richard Williamson (b. before 1650 — d. 1721, Princess Anne County, Virginia)
        • Mary Kempe (d. Unknown)
      • (Parents: George and Mary Hutchinson Kempe — Kempe’s Landing, Princess Anne County, Virginia)
      • ————–Gen 3 Mary Williamson
          • Robert Berry (b. Unknown — d. 1730; parents unknown)
        • ————————Gen 4 Robert Berry (b. 1729 — d. 1814, Fiddleton Plantation, Orange County, NC)
            • Elizabeth Cate (b. 1735 — d. aft. 1816, Fiddleton Plantation, Orange County, NC)
          • (Parents: John and Margery Lawrence Cate)

Children of Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry (Gen 5)#

  • ————————————Gen 5 Mary Berry (b. about 1759)
  • ————————————Gen 5 Robert Berry Jr. (b. about 1760)
  • ————————————Gen 5 John Berry (b. about 1762)
  • ————————————Gen 5 Joshua Berry (b. about 1766)
  • ————————————Gen 5 Isaac Berry (b. about 1768)
  • ————————————Gen 5 Elizabeth Berry (b. about 1770)
  • ————————————Gen 5 Thomas Berry (b. about 1772)
  • ————————————Gen 5 David J. Berry (b. about 1774)
  • ————————————Gen 5 William Berry (b. about 1775)
  • ————————————Gen 5 Henry Berry (b. 1776)

Other Children of Richard and Mary Kempe Williamson#

  • ————–Gen 3 John Williamson
  • ————–Gen 3 Roger Williamson
  • ————–Gen 3 Bartholomew Williamson
  • ————–Gen 3 George Williamson
  • ————–Gen 3 Sarah Williamson
      • Matthew Mathias
  • ————–Gen 3 Ann Williamson
      • Hugh Whitehurst

Other Children of Ann Foster and Roger Williamson#

  • ——–Gen 2 Bartholomew Williamson (b. before 1650)
  • ——–Gen 2 Sarah Williamson (b. before 1650)

Ancestor List#

  • George Kempe — Great Grandfather of Robert Berry (OC) — was born in England
  • Mary Hutchinson Kempe — Great Grandmother of Robert Berry (OC)
  • Roger Williamson — Great Grandfather of Robert Berry (OC)
  • Ann Foster Williamson — Great Grandmother of Robert Berry (OC) — born in England in 1620 and died in Princess Anne County, Virginia about 1692
  • Richard Williamson — Grandfather of Robert Berry (OC)
  • Mary Kempe Williamson — Grandmother of Robert Berry (OC)
  • Robert Berry (PAC) — Father of Robert Berry (OC) — died in Princess Anne County, Virginia in 1730
  • Mary Williamson Berry — Mother of Robert Berry (OC) — died in Princess Anne County, Virginia about 1746

Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Joshua Berry × Nancy Ellison — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 4Eli Berry × Judy Betsy Taylor — Orange County, North Carolina

Generation 4 Eli Berry

Born
October 10, 1821, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
August 15, 1900, Cedar Grove, Orange County, North Carolina
Married
Judy Betsy Taylor (married December 21, 1846; born November 27, 1823; died July 31, 1900)
Parents
Joshua Berry and Nancy Ellison Berry, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Eli and Judy Betsy Taylor Berry#

Eli Berry was born in Orange County on October 10 1821 and died in Cedar Grove, Orange County NC, on August 15 1900. He married Judy Betsy Taylor who was born on November 27 1823 and died in Cedar Grove, Orange County NC, on July 31 1900. The couple Married on 21 Dec 1846.

item.title: Frederick M. Berry Iowa
item.description:
Frederick M. Berry (1874–1951)

Son of Harrison and Henrietta Havens Berry, Linn County, Iowa, to Polk County, Wisconsin.

Frederick M. Berry, son of Harrison and Henrietta Havens Berry#

Ancestors#

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. Generation Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  4. Generation Joshua Berry Jr. and Anna Thorpe Berry: Ohio
  5. Generation Lewis and Elizabeth Haywood Berry: Indiana and Kansas
  6. Generation Harrison and Henrietta Havens Berry: Iowa and Colorado

FREDERICK M. BERRY was born 28 February 1874 in Palo, Linn County, Iowa to Harrison and Henrietta (Havens) Berry. When Fred was four years old in 1878, his mother Henrietta died. According to the newspaper article in the Vinton Eagle: “Her husband was cutting a large tree down close to where they lived, when Henrietta attempted to go near where he was to tell him that she was going to a neighbors, having her babe in her arms and a little girl running along with her, when the tree fell, crushing her almost into a jelly, while remarkable as it may seem, the baby still lives, though probably fatally injured. The little girl too, narrowly escaped.” RosaBelle did survive, but Henrietta died instantly and baby Eliza died within three days.

item.title: Harrison Berry Iowa
item.description:
Harrison Berry (1854–1934)

Son of Lewis and Elizabeth Haywood Berry, Benton County, Iowa, to Pueblo, Colorado.

Harrison Berry, Son Of Lewis And Elizabeth Haywood Berry#

Ancestors#

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. Generation Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  4. Generation Joshua Berry Jr. and Anna Thorpe Berry: Ohio
  5. Generation Lewis and Elizabeth Haywood Berry: Indiana and Kansas

Harrison Berry was born 31 May 1854 in Benton County, Iowa to Lewis and Eliza (Hayward) Berry. He was named after his uncle Harrison Berry, the son of Joshua and Anna (Thorpe) Berry, the brother of his father Lewis. He was the second child born to Lewis and Eliza, who were united in marriage on 18 March 1851 in Boone County, Indiana. Lewis and Eliza, along with Lewis’s parents Joshua and Anna (Thorpe) Berry and Eliza’s father [Abijah Hayward] had moved from Indiana to Iowa prior to the birth of Harrison. They all engaged in farming in the Benton and Linn County, Iowa area.

item.title: James Berry Tenn
item.description:
James Berry, son of Joshua Berry and Nancy Ellison Berry#

  • Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  • Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
  • Gen 3 Joshua Berry × Nancy Ellison — Orange County, North Carolina

James Berry was born in 1800 in Orange County, North Carolina. James Berry and Mary Parker were married in North Carolina about 1829. Their first three daughters were born in North Carolina. The oldest daughter, Nancy, was born in 1830. The youngest daughter, Delila, was born in North Carolina in 1836 and was 14 years old in the 1850 Smith County, Tennessee census. The oldest child born in Tennessee was, Martha, who was 11 years old in 1850. This means James and Mary were in North Carolina in 1836 and in Tennessee in 1839. I have no absolute proof that the James Berry on this page is related to us but all the facts indicate he was..

Robert Berry’s Deed to Joshua Berry with James Berry as a witness. Dated February 24 1811 (Orange County Deed Book 14 Page 57)

Orange County, NC Census Listings, 1790–1830

item.title: Joshua Berry Jr. Ohio
item.description:
Joshua Berry Jr. — Ohio Detail

Biography and Census Records, Orange County NC to Benton County Iowa

Joshua Berry Jr., Son Of Joshua Berry And Nancy Ellison Berry#

ANCESTORS:

  • Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia

  • Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.

item.title: Joshua Jr. Census
item.description:
Joshua Berry Jr. — Census Records

Census Listings from Ohio, Indiana, and Iowa, 1830–1880

Ancestors:

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. Generation Joshua Jr. and Anna Thorpe Berry: NC, Ohio, Benton Co. Iowa.

Transcribed Joshua Berry Jr. 1830 Gallia County, Ohio census. James Thorpe in this census is most likely Annah Thorp’s sister.

item.title: Ken Berry
item.description:
Ancestors:

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry, Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry, Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. Generation Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry, Orange County, North Carolina.
  4. Generation Lewis and Sally Toler Berry, Orange County, North Carolina.
  5. Generation Ira and Nancy Wynn Berry, Union County, Kentucky.
  6. Generation Franklin Pierce and Sophia Sellers Berry. Kentucky.
  7. Generation Theodore Guest and Verda Hall Berry, Kentucky, Williamson Co. Illinois.
  8. Generation Eugene Darrell and Bernice Larson Berry Moline Illinois.
  9. Generation Kenneth Ronald Berry.

Ken Berry’s 3rd great grandfather Ira Berry was born in Orange County, North Carolina. Ira Berry and his sister Nancy Berry Waggoner’s young family left North Carolina in 1855. Ira was a single man at the time. He met Nancy Wynn on his journey to Kentucky. They were married about 1855 and listed in the 1860 Union County, Kentucky census with their three young children. The oldest was Franklin who was born in Kentucky in 1856. Ira Berry’s sisters family was living next door. Ira’s Father, Lewis Berry, died in Orange County NC from the Flu epidemic in 1858 and Ira’s Mother Sally Toler Berry was living with her daughter Nancy Waggoner in this 1860 Census.

item.title: Lewis Berry NC
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Lewis Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Joshua Berry × Nancy Ellison — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 4Lewis Berry × Sally Tolar — Orange County, North Carolina

Generation 4 Lewis Berry

Born
1805, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
1858, Orange County, North Carolina
Married
Sally Tolar (married October 13, 1829)
Parents
Joshua Berry and Nancy Ellison Berry, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Lewis Berry, Son Of Joshua Berry And Nancy Ellison Berry#

Lewis Berry was born in 1805 in Orange County North Carolina.

item.title: Lewis Berry Ohio
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Lewis Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Joshua Berry Sr. × Nancy Ellison — Orange County, NC → Ohio
Gen 4Joshua Berry Jr. × Anna Thorpe — Gallia County, Ohio
Gen 5Lewis Berry × Eliza Haywood — Ohio → Iowa → Wichita, Kansas

Generation 5 Lewis Berry

Born
April 27, 1826, Gallia County, Ohio
Died
March 6, 1907, Wichita, Kansas
Married
Eliza Haywood (married March 20, 1851)
Parents
Joshua Berry Jr. and Anna Thorpe Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

LEWIS BERRY was born on April 27th, 1826 in Gallia Ohio. Lewis Berry married Eliza Haywood who was also born in Preble County Ohio on November 16, 1833. Lewis and Eliza were married on March 20th, 1851, Squire Bowen of Thorton, Indiana officiated. Elizabeth Haywood was the daughter of Abijah and Naomi Haywood of Essex County, N.J. Elizabeth’s mother died in 1851 and her father Abijah was living with Lewis and Elizabeth in their 1860 and 1870 census listing.

item.title: Mary Kempe PAC
item.description:
Mary Kempe of Princess Anne County

Daughter of George and Mary Hutchinson Kempe, married Richard Williamson.

Mary Kempe from Princess Ann County Virginia#

PARENTS:

Mary’s Parents George and Mary Hutchinson Kempe

MARY KEMPE married RICHARD WILLIAMSON (daughter of George and Mary Hutchinson Kempe of Kempe’s Landing) There is some question at this time about Mary Kempe’s marriage date since George and Mary Hutchison Kempe may not have arrived in America before 1650. My thoughts are that Richard Williamson and Mary Kempe were probably married around 1670 to 1675. From Richard Williamson’s 1721 will we learn that his children were:

item.title: Nancy Berry Spinster Joshua's daughter
item.description:
Nancy Berry, daughter of Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry#

Ancestors#

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia

  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.

  3. Generation Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.

  4. Nancy Berry born 1810 and died in March 1883: Orange County, North Carolina. Nancy Berry was never married and died with leaving a will. Nancy Berry’s nephew Eli D. Berry was appointed Administrator of Nancy Berry’s Estate.

item.title: Proof Page
item.description:
Proof of the Berry-Williamson Connection

Evidence linking Robert Berry of Orange County, NC, to Princess Anne County, Virginia, through the Williamson and Kempe families.

This page assembles the documentary evidence connecting Robert Berry of Orange County, NC, to Princess Anne County, Virginia, through shared surnames, tax lists, and land records.

item.title: Robert Berry Joshua's Son NC
item.description:
Robert Berry, Joshua's Son

Son of Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry, Orange County, North Carolina. Married Sally Cates.

Robert Berry, son of Joshua Berry and Nancy Ellison Berry#

Ancestors#

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. Generation Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.

ROBERT BERRY was born in 1800 in Orange County North Carolina. Robert Berry and Sally Cates were married in Person County North Carolina circa 1814. There is a great deal that we did not know about this family. We know that they did live in Orange County for their entire lives or at least until 1850. Sally Berry age 52 (born 1798) listed as head of household in 1850 with her two daughters Rachel Berry age 11 (born 1839) and Sarah Berry age 14 (born 1836).

item.title: William Cate's Estate Sale
item.description:
William Cate's Estate Sale — 1803

Items purchased by Robert Berry Sr. and family at the estate sale of their father-in-law, William Cate.

Below is a list of items that Robert Berry Sen, and his family bought at William Cate’s Estate Sale in 1803. William and Elizabeth Smith (Messersmitt) Cate were Hannah and Sarah Cates’ parents and William and Thomas Berry’s father and mother-in-law.
